mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or joint positive signals yield negative returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes or Rank IC drops below 0.01 when inflation exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a stable baseline for consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day returns or predictive accuracy falls below random chance over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches the 220 threshold, persistent inflation expectations trigger aggressive central bank tightening fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 220.0 fails to predict negative returns or market prices in the threshold effect within 48 hours.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales reveals underlying demand resilience that outpaces headline economic prints, driving positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0% or retail sales show consecutive monthly declines.

mechanism_narrative: A decoupling where industrial production declines while payroll employment remains elevated signals rising unit labor costs and margin compression, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The decoupling pattern fails to predict negative returns or margin compression does not materialize in earnings reports within 90 days.
